channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Brawl over Pokémon cards breaks out at California Costco
item.description: The fight, captured on video by X user DisguisedToast, unfolded on Jan. 16 at the Costco in Atwater Village, the Los Angeles Times reported.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the escalating conflict in eastern Congo as rebels close in on Goma
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Fighting has escalated sharply in recent weeks in eastern Congo, where rebels have seized key towns and are closing in on the city of Goma, the government’s last stronghold in the region bordering Rwanda.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mediterranean diet improves brain memory: Study
item.description: A Mediterranean diet is beneficial for boosting brain function according to a new study by Tulane University.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: State Department freezes new funding for nearly all US aid programs worldwide
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department ordered a sweeping freeze Friday on new funding for almost all U.S. foreign assistance, making exceptions for emergency food programs and military aid to Israel and Egypt. The order threatened a quick halt to many of the billions of dollars in U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: SZA to join Kendrick Lamar for Super Bowl LIX halftime show
item.description: Apple Music released a teaser trailer on Thursday showing the Compton native on the gridiron, assessing the field before the big game while he's talking to someone on the phone.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal agencies begin removing DEI guidance from websites in Trump crackdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal agencies have begun removing resources for underrepresented Americans from their webpages following President Donald Trump's executive order cracking down on diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives.
item.dc:creator: ALIA WONG and ANNIE MA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Las Vegas Raiders are negotiating a deal to hire Pete Carroll as their head coach, AP source says
item.description: The Las Vegas Raiders are negotiating a deal to hire Pete Carroll to be their new head coach, a person with knowledge of the decision told The Associated Press on Friday. The person spoke on condition of anonymity because the contract hasn’t been finalized.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: Democrat state senator handcuffed after refusing to show driver's license at traffic stop
item.description: Body camera footage captured Oklahoma State Sen. Regina Goodwin's "very verbal" exchange with a police officer after she failed to hand over her license after being pulled over.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Desperate families await return of 4 female soldiers held by Hamas for 15 months
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Hamas announced Friday that it would release four female soldiers held hostage for 15 months in Gaza, as part of an exchange for dozens of Palestinian prisoners laid out in its ceasefire agreement with Israel.
item.dc:creator: SHLOMO MOR and WAAFA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump cites voter ID, water flow as conditions for LA wildfire aid
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday said he wanted to see two actions taken in California before he offered federal support for Los Angeles as it grapples with wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Target latest company to roll back DEI programs
item.description: Target has announced it is rolling back its diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) policies, becoming the latest corporation to do so following President Donald Trump’s election.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oklahoma wants federal inmate transferred so he can be put to death
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Oklahoma's top prosecutor asked the federal Bureau of Prisons to transfer an inmate to state custody so that he could be executed for his role in the kidnapping and killing of a 77-year-old woman in 1999.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What JFK assassination files are still classified? Trump’s order could bring them to light
item.description: Millions of documents related to the 1963 assassination of President John F. Kennedy in Dallas have already been made public, but President Donald Trump has ordered the release of thousands of still-classified files.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Stengle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Spirit Airlines updates dress code, tattoo policy for passengers
item.description: Passengers flying on Spirit Airlines can now be removed from a flight if a tattoo or other "body art" is deemed "lewd, obscene, or offensive.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Homeowners in Palisades Fire area angered by bank's decision
item.description: With the devastating Palisades Fire still smoldering in California, Lisa Pelton and some of her neighbors in Mandeville Canyon received an unpleasant notice from their bank: Their home equity lines of credit were being slashed.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: Target is ending its diversity goals as a strong DEI opponent occupies the White House
item.description: Discount store chain Target says it’s joining rival Walmart and a number of other prominent American brands in scaling back corporate diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Anne D'Innocenzio, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Homes swatted in California; Officers taunted through Ring camera
item.description: A man pleaded guilty to reporting false emergencies to police, then taunting officers using residences' Ring cameras.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation

item.title: 80 Guatemalan migrants deported from Biggs Army Airfield
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) — The U.S. Department of Defense, along with U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), deported 80 Guatemalan migrants on Friday morning, Jan. 24, from Biggs Army Airfield at Fort Bliss, U.S. Rep. Tony Gonzales, R-Texas said in a post on X.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Luna, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about the ruling blocking Trump's order on birthright citizenship
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — President Donald Trump's executive order denying U.S. citizenship to the children of parents living in the country illegally has faced the first of what will be many legal tests. It didn't fare well.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Asylum-seekers pushed to new extremes in Mexico after Trump's border crackdown begins
item.description: ATOTONILCO DE TULA, Mexico (AP) — When Dayana Castro heard that the U.S. asylum appointment she waited over a year for was canceled in an instant, she had no doubt: She was heading north any way she could.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY and EDGAR H. CLEMENTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mississippi state lawmaker introduces 'Contraception Begins at Erection Act' with fines for masturbation
item.description: A Democratic Mississippi state senator introduced legislation this week that makes it unlawful for men to masturbate "without the intent to fertilize an embryo," with the lawmaker criticizing anti-abortion measures that only "focus on the woman’s role.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Illegal immigrant admitted to killing woman on her 21st birthday
item.description: Ecuadorian native Jhon Moises Chacaguasay-Ilbis pleaded guilty to murdering 21-year-old Jocelyn Jhoana Toaquiza at a Syracuse, New York, Airbnb on her 21st birthday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House says migrant deportation flights with military aircraft have begun
item.description: The Trump administration has started flying immigrants who entered the U.S. illegally out of the country using military aircraft, a White House spokesperson said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: London court clears way to extradite US mother accused of killing 2 of her children in Colorado
item.description: A London judge has rejected a U.S. mother’s challenge to her extradition to Colorado to face murder charges in the deaths of two of her young children.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Attorney Ben Crump files lawsuits against California power company
item.description: Lawsuits allege Southern California Edison failed to turn off utilities despite dangerous conditions.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Flame thrower and 'flusher' truck: Southern cities get creative to clear snow, ice
item.description: Days after a winter storm dropped ice and record-breaking snow, cleanup efforts were underway Thursday in several major Southern cities such as New Orleans, where crews were removing snow the same way they remove trash, drink cups and plastic beads after Mardi Gras.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ford recalls over 270,000 Broncos and Mavericks: Here's why
item.description: Ford Motor Co. is recalling 272,827 Broncos and Mavericks due to a power problem that can cause vehicles to stop unexpectedly or fail to start.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: President Trump visiting storm-battered towns 'treated badly' by Dems and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to visit California after ripping 'idiot' Newsom on wildfire; critics bash crime, homelessness, spending
item.description: President Trump is ripping California Gov. Gavin Newsom over mismanagement of the state leading up to the devastating wildfires and handling of sanctuary cities ahead of his visit to the Golden State.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Resale prices for iPhones 'with TikTok installed' soar up to $50,000
item.description: TikTok is no longer available in the App Store as of Thursday, however there's still a way to get back on the platform – for a price.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KTLA 5

item.title: Active-duty troops begin arriving at US-Mexico border in Texas and California to combat migrant crisis
item.description: Active-duty military personnel began arriving along the southern border Thursday evening after President Donald Trump declared a national emergency to address the immigration crisis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Illegal immigrant in Texas wanted for allegedly raping child in Mexico repatriated back to native country
item.description: An illegal immigrant wanted in Mexico for allegedly raping a child was repatriated back to his country after entering the U.S. four times illegally since 2002.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police review writings tied to Nashville school shooter who killed female student and himself
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The investigation into why a Nashville high school student fatally shot a classmate before killing himself has centered on his online writings, which authorities describe as concerning warning signs.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Convicted California killer sings in front of victims' families
item.description: A man convicted of murdering two women in 1986 interrupted his own sentencing hearing on Tuesday by singing in front of his victims' families.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Florida officer accidentally shoots man with his own gun
item.description: The officer "had difficulty removing" the gun, and while trying to do so, it fired, striking the driver in the leg, the sheriff's office said.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: LA wildfires: Surveillance video captures suspects stealing fire equipment during disaster response
item.description: Federal investigators are searching for two men who were caught on video stealing critical fire equipment during the Los Angeles wildfire response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
